title: Pioglitazone -- Contraindications
content: Patients with a known hypersensitivity to pioglitazone or any of its components Diabetic ketoacidosis or type 1 diabetes, since pioglitazone is active only in the presence of insulin. Hypoglycemia, therefore, regular blood sugar monitoring is imperative Initiation of therapy in patients with established New York Heart Association (NYHA) Class III or IV heart failure is a contraindication in the US Symptomatic heart failure at any stage (NYHA Class I, II, III, IV) is contraindicated in Canada Any evidence of fluid overload Active (current) bladder cancer or history of bladder cancer, uninvestigated microscopic hematuria History of fracture or at high risk for fracture Active liver disease, liver transaminase  levels more than 2.5 times above the upper limit of normal Pregnancy The Beers Criteria lists pioglitazone as a potentially inappropriate medication (PIM) for the geriatric population with heart or kidney failure.

content: Family history is vital and provides some of the most useful information. Patients with MODY have a strong family history of diabetes spanning at least 3 generations. title: Insulinoma -- History and Physical
content: Fasting hypoglycemia is the most characteristic finding of insulinoma, reported in approximately 73% of cases. About 20% of patients experience both fasting and postprandial hypoglycemic symptoms. Additionally, an increasing number of patients have been reporting exclusively postprandial hypoglycemic symptoms, which appear to be more common in men. Weight gain is a common finding in most patients with insulinoma. [13]

content: When insulin is self-injected, laboratory studies will show high plasma insulin levels in combination with low plasma C-peptide levels during the hypoglycemic episode. title: Factitious Hypoglycemia -- History and Physical
content: Taking a good history remains a crucial step in evaluating hypoglycemic patients. Factitious hypoglycemia should be a potential diagnosis in the differential for patients who work in the medical profession, who are in close contact with diabetic individuals, and those with underlying psychiatric disorders like major depressive disorder and a history of suicide attempts. It is also essential to review the patient's current medication list, including any herbal preparations, as some herbals may be contaminated with sulfonylureas. It should also be suspected in diabetic individuals who, despite repeated adjustments of their insulin or oral hypoglycemic doses, continue to present with recurrent hypoglycemia episodes.

title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2721
content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes ارتفاع السكر في الدم, اعتلال عصبي سكري, فرط أنسولين الدم, and ورم غلوكاغوني. ارتفاع السكر في الدم (high blood sugar) is a primary manifestation of diabetes, reflecting impaired glucose regulation by the endocrine pancreas. اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy) is a common chronic complication of prolonged hyperglycemia, affecting peripheral nerves and leading to sensory and motor deficits. فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia) denotes excessive insulin secretion, which can occur in early insulin resistance or insulin‑producing tumors and may precede or accompany diabetes. ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma) is a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, often resulting in persistent hyperglycemia and weight loss. Recognizing how these conditions are interrelated within endocrine pancreatic disease supports comprehensive diagnosis, monitoring, and therapeutic strategies for affected patients.

title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1875
content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes several related disorders: ارتفاع السكر في الدم (elevated blood glucose), مرض السكري دون مضاعفات (diabetes without complications), اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy), فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia), and ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma). content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad classification that encompasses several distinct conditions. Within this category, مرض السكري دون مضاعفات refers to diabetes without complications, representing a form of the disease where hyperglycemia is present but secondary complications have not yet developed. فرط أنسولين الدم denotes hyperinsulinemia, a state of elevated insulin levels in the blood that can arise from pancreatic beta‑cell overactivity or insulin‑secreting tumors and may lead to hypoglycemia. ورم غلوكاغوني describes glucagonoma, a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, causing characteristic metabolic disturbances such as glucose intolerance, weight loss, and a distinctive skin rash. title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2046
content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES encompasses a spectrum of related conditions. It includes ارتفاع السكر في الدم, which denotes elevated blood glucose levels, and مرض السكري دون مضاعفات, representing diabetes without complications. Neurological involvement is reflected by اعتلال عصبي سكري, a diabetic neuropathy. Facial manifestations may appear as ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي, a progressive hemi‑facial atrophy that can be associated with pancreatic endocrine disorders. Metabolic dysregulation is further illustrated by فرط أنسولين الدم, an excess of circulating insulin, and by ورم غلوكاغوني, a glucagon‑producing tumor of the pancreas. Together these entities illustrate the diverse clinical presentations and complications that arise from diseases of the endocrine pancreas, highlighting the importance of comprehensive assessment and management in affected patients.

title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 1267
content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes several specific pancreatic endocrine disorders. Among its children are ارتفاع السكر في الدم, which describes the elevated blood‑glucose levels that characterize hyperglycemia; مرض السكري دون مضاعفات, which refers to diabetes that has not yet produced secondary complications; فرط أنسولين الدم, indicating an excess of circulating insulin often seen in early insulin‑resistance states or insulin‑producing tumors; and ورم غلوكاغوني, a rare glucagon‑secreting tumor (glucagonoma) that can also cause hyperglycemia. These conditions are interrelated through their impact on pancreatic hormone production and regulation of blood glucose, highlighting the importance of comprehensive assessment and management of pancreatic endocrine function in patient care.

title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1889
content: Sulfonylureas for blood glucose lowering are a class of sulfonyl‑containing compounds (sulfonylmočovinové sloučeniny) that function as oral hypoglycemic agents by stimulating pancreatic insulin secretion. Within this pharmacologic group, glibenklamid and glimepiride are individual members that share the common mechanism of enhancing insulin release, thereby helping to control elevated blood glucose levels in patients with diabetes. Understanding that these agents belong to the broader sulfonyl‑compound family and recognizing the specific drugs glibenklamid and glimepiride facilitates appropriate selection and management of sulfonylurea therapy in clinical practice.

title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 2063
content: Glucagon-like peptide‑1 (GLP‑1) analogues constitute a class of therapeutic agents that mimic the incretin hormone GLP‑1, enhancing glucose‑dependent insulin secretion, inhibiting glucagon release, slowing gastric emptying, and promoting satiety. Liraglutide is a member of this GLP‑1 analogue class, and its pharmacologic actions make it valuable for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus and for weight management in patients with obesity.

title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1756
content: The category “DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES” encompasses a spectrum of disorders and clinical manifestations that arise from dysfunction of the pancreatic endocrine system. It includes “ارتفاع السكر في الدم” (hyperglycemia), which represents elevated blood‑glucose levels and is a primary feature of diabetes. “غيبوبة فرط الأسمولية السكري” (diabetic hyperosmolar coma) is a severe, acute complication of uncontrolled hyperglycemia characterized by extreme dehydration and altered mental status. “مرض السكري دون مضاعفات” (diabetes mellitus without complications) denotes diabetes in its uncomplicated form, serving as the baseline condition from which other sequelae may develop. Among the chronic complications, “اعتلال عصبي سكري” (diabetic neuropathy) reflects nerve damage caused by prolonged hyperglycemia, leading to sensory and motor deficits. “ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي” (progressive hemifacial atrophy) is listed as a possible extra‑pancreatic manifestation that can be associated with underlying endocrine pancreatic disease. Metabolic dysregulation is further illustrated by “فرط أنسولين الدم” (hyperinsulinemia), an excess of circulating insulin that may precede or accompany insulin resistance. Finally, “ورم غلوكاغوني” (glucagonoma) represents a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, producing a distinct clinical syndrome. Together, these entities illustrate the diverse acute, chronic, and neoplastic conditions linked to diseases of the endocrine pancreas, highlighting the importance of comprehensive assessment and management of both glycemic control and associated complications.

title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2904
content: Diabetic retinopathy (اعتِلال الشبكية السُّكَّرِيّ) is clinically associated with end‑stage renal disease (مرض كلوي في المرحلة النهائية). Both conditions represent severe microvascular complications of long‑standing diabetes mellitus, and the presence of diabetic retinopathy often signals an increased risk of progression to advanced kidney failure. Recognizing this association is essential for clinicians, as early detection and management of retinal changes can prompt more aggressive monitoring of renal function, and coordinated care can help mitigate the morbidity associated with simultaneous vision loss and the need for renal replacement therapy.

title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 194
content: The classification “DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES” is situated within the broader category of “other endocrine disorders.” Within this group, several specific conditions are identified as subtypes or manifestations: “ارتفاع السكر في الدم” (hyperglycemia) represents elevated blood glucose levels; “مرض السكري دون مضاعفات” denotes diabetes mellitus without complications; “نقص تركيز السكر” (hypoglycemia) reflects abnormally low blood glucose and is also recognized as a component of the wider “other endocrine disorders” category; “فرط أنسولين الدم” (hyperinsulinemia) describes excessive circulating insulin; “ورم غلوكاغوني” (glucagonoma) is a pancreatic tumor that secretes glucagon; and “اضطراب إفراز الجسترين” (gastrin secretion disorder) indicates abnormal gastrin release. The gastrin secretion disorder further leads to “الحثل وإضطرابات الإفراز في الغشاء المخاطي المَعِدِيّ المِعَوِيّ” (gastric mucosal atrophy and associated secretion abnormalities), illustrating a downstream effect on the gastric mucosa. Together, these relationships delineate how diabetes‑related endocrine pancreatic diseases encompass a spectrum of hyper‑ and hypoglycemic states, hormone‑excess conditions, neoplastic processes, and secondary gastrointestinal disturbances, all nested within the larger framework of endocrine disorders.
